qa_pairs.question: What is the primary purpose of this page?
qa_pairs.answer: This page explains issues related to transferring your data between our facilities and our regional partners.

qa_pairs.question: Whom should I contact if I have any doubts about the data transfer advice?
qa_pairs.answer: If you are in any doubt about details of the following advice, contact our technical support for help.

qa_pairs.question: What should I ascertain before starting a data migration?
qa_pairs.answer: Make sure you know whether you are responsible for your own data migration, or whether our staff will be migrating your data.

qa_pairs.question: Who should I contact if I am unsure about migration responsibilities?
qa_pairs.answer: If you are in any doubt, contact our technical support.

qa_pairs.question: What steps should I take if I am unfamiliar with Globus before migration?
qa_pairs.answer: If you haven't used Globus before, read about it now and verify that it works on the system you are migrating from.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it important to test data migration tools like tar, gzip, or zip on test data?
qa_pairs.answer: Test any other tools you will use (like tar, gzip, zip) on test data to ensure you know how they work before using them on important data.

qa_pairs.question: Should I delay starting my data migration until the last minute?
qa_pairs.answer: No, do not wait until the last minute to start your migration.

qa_pairs.question: How long should I expect a transfer of hundreds of gigabytes to take?
qa_pairs.answer: Expect hundreds of gigabytes to take hours to transfer, but give yourself days in case there is a problem.

qa_pairs.question: What is the expected transfer time for terabytes of data?
qa_pairs.answer: Expect terabytes to take days.

qa_pairs.question: Why is cleaning up files and directories recommended before a major data migration?
qa_pairs.answer: A major data migration is a good reminder to clean up your files and directories because moving less data will take less time, and storage space on new systems should not be wasted.

qa_pairs.question: What types of files should I delete if I compile programs and keep source code?
qa_pairs.answer: If you compile programs and keep source code, delete any intermediate files like those handled by `make clean`, `make realclean`, or `rm *.o`.

qa_pairs.question: What are large files named like `core.12345`, and can they be deleted?
qa_pairs.answer: If you find any large files named like `core.12345` and you don't know that they are, they are probably core dumps and can be deleted.

qa_pairs.question: How do file transfer programs handle many small files versus a single large file?
qa_pairs.answer: Most file transfer programs move one file of a reasonable size more efficiently than thousands of small files of equal total size.

qa_pairs.question: What tool can I use to combine directories with many small files for more efficient transfer?
qa_pairs.answer: If you have directories or directory trees containing many small files, use tar to combine (archive) them.

qa_pairs.question: When can large files benefit from compression?
qa_pairs.answer: Large files can benefit from compression in some cases, especially text files which can usually be compressed a great deal.

qa_pairs.question: Does compressing and then decompressing a file always save transfer time?
qa_pairs.answer: No, compressing a file only for the purpose of transferring it, and then decompressing it at the end of the transfer will not necessarily save time, as it depends on compressibility, compression time, and bandwidth.

qa_pairs.question: What tools can I use if I decide compression is worthwhile for my files?
qa_pairs.answer: If you decide compression is worthwhile, you can use tar or gzip for this.

qa_pairs.question: What advice is given regarding duplicate data when migrating from multiple systems?
qa_pairs.answer: Try not to move the same data twice; if you have data duplicated on multiple sources, choose one and only move the duplicate data from that one.

qa_pairs.question: What should I be cautious about regarding files with duplicate names during migration?
qa_pairs.answer: Beware of files with duplicate names but different content, and ensure you will not accidentally overwrite one file with another of the same name.

qa_pairs.question: Which tool is recommended for file transfer during migration if supported at the source site?
qa_pairs.answer: If it is supported at your source site, use Globus to set up your file transfer.

qa_pairs.question: What are the benefits of using Globus for file transfers?
qa_pairs.answer: Globus is the most user-friendly and efficient tool for this task and is designed to recover from network interruptions automatically.

qa_pairs.question: What setting should I enable in Globus's 'Transfer & Timer Options'?
qa_pairs.answer: We recommend you enable the setting to 'preserve source file modification times' in the 'Transfer & Timer Options'.

qa_pairs.question: If Globus is not supported at my source site, what data management practices become more crucial?
qa_pairs.answer: If Globus is not supported at your source site, then compressing data and avoiding duplication is even more important.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I am using `scp`, `sftp`, or `rsync` for data migration?
qa_pairs.answer: Make a schedule to migrate your data in blocks of a few hundreds of GBs at a time.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it beneficial to migrate data in blocks when using tools like `scp` or `rsync`?
qa_pairs.answer: Migrating data in blocks means if the transfer stops, you can restart from an incomplete file without re-transferring files that are already complete.

qa_pairs.question: How can I monitor the progress of a data transfer when using `scp`, `sftp`, or `rsync`?
qa_pairs.answer: Check regularly to see that the transfer process has not stopped; file size is a good indicator of progress.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if a transfer process appears to have stopped for several minutes?
qa_pairs.answer: If no files have changed size for several minutes, then something may have gone wrong. If restarting the transfer does not work, contact our technical support.

qa_pairs.question: What general advice is given about the duration of large data transfers?
qa_pairs.answer: Be patient. Even with Globus, transferring large volumes of data can be time consuming.
